title: Please update to golang 1.19 or 1.20
body:
### Issue Description

1.18 does not receive security fixes anymore. And there is at least one critical vulnerability [CVE-2023-24538](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-24538)

comments.body: @chrisbulgaria i was just going to close this as inadequate information provided, but I guess I'd like to know exactly what you want here?

hi ,
unless i misunderstood something podman is currently built with go 1.18.9 - meaning podman includes the go stdlibs which don't get any security updates anymore. (backport are only to the last 2 major versions ). therefore podman includes an increasing amount of vulnerabilities - among those the ones cited above . therefore i suggest to upgrade :)

Hi @chrisbulgaria, the missing link here is the output of `podman info` (as requested in the template). Could you please provide that? It seems possible that you are using an old podman build, or a distro that is not under our control. If you don't provide the information requested in the template, there is really nothing we can do to understand your concern.
@chrisbulgaria What build are you talking about?

Hi,
ok, Im very sorry, I seem to use an older version, see below,
But podman latest code still seems to use go 1.18 ? 
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/91da62d74c82bf6c30245c4d49faa902102b00f5/go.mod#L3
But yes, will close it , sorry 

version:
  APIVersion: 4.2.0
  Built: 1677014962
  BuiltTime: Tue Feb 21 21:29:22 2023
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.18.9
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.2.0

The go.mod file version lists the minimum go version required to build podman based on the go features that we are using. It is automatically managed by the go toolchain. Unless we start using 1.19+ features there is no reason to update the version in go.mod.
